web.description: Android Code Search is a tool that helps you view the Android source code as it's laid out when you actually use it.
web.markdown:

- [Documentation](https://source.android.com/docs)

Effective in 2026, to align with our trunk stable development model and ensure platform stability for the ecosystem, we will publish source code to AOSP in Q2 and Q4. For building and contributing to AOSP, we recommend utilizing `android-latest-release` instead of `aosp-main`. The `android-latest-release` manifest branch will always reference the most recent release pushed to AOSP. For more information, see [Changes to AOSP](https://source.android.com/docs/whatsnew/site-updates#aosp-changes).

- [AOSP](https://source.android.com/)
- [Docs](https://source.android.com/docs)
- [Getting Started](https://source.android.com/docs/setup)

Was this helpful?

# Android Code search    Stay organized with collections      Save and categorize content based on your preferences.

The Android Open Source Project (AOSP) is stored as collection of Git
repositories, which are managed together using the
[Repo](https://source.android.com/docs/setup/reference/repo) tool. Most Android development tools (such as
GitHub) can't see the source code the way that it's laid out when it's checked
out. [_Android Code Search_](https://cs.android.com/) is a tool that
helps you view the Android source code as it's laid out when you actually use
it.

Android Code Search makes it easy to navigate cross-references across all of
AOSP by letting you to click through one part of the source code to another.
This method of navigation helps you switch between Android's open source
branches.

Android Code Search is similar to Google Code Search. For more details on using
Google Code Search, see
[Getting started with Code Search](https://developers.google.com/code-search/user/getting-started).

Was this helpful?

web.title: Android Open Source Project
web.description: Read about the Android Open Source Project (AOSP) and learn how to develop, customize, and test your devices.

[![Android Open Source Project](https://www.gstatic.com/devrel-devsite/prod/va845a6a69ec71f6762e80b2da8e8faa65e74307aa7e53d6c2485adee73edb48b/androidsource/images/lockup.png)](https://source.android.com/)

[Sign in](https://source.android.com/_d/signin?continue=https%3A%2F%2Fsource.android.com%2F&prompt=select_account)

# Android   Open Source Project

Use the Android operating system to power your device.

`/`

Effective in 2026, to align with our trunk stable development model and ensure platform stability for the ecosystem, we will publish source code to AOSP in Q2 and Q4. For building and contributing to AOSP, we recommend utilizing `android-latest-release` instead of `aosp-main`. The `android-latest-release` manifest branch will always reference the most recent release pushed to AOSP. For more information, see [Changes to AOSP](https://source.android.com/docs/whatsnew/site-updates#aosp-changes).

- [AOSP](https://source.android.com/)

[![](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/16/images/android-16-logo.svg)](https://source.android.com/docs/whatsnew/android-16-release)

Featured

### [Android 16 QPR2 is available!](https://source.android.com/docs/whatsnew/android-16-release)

Learn about the features added in Android 16 QPR2 and how to implement them on your devices.



[Read more](https://source.android.com/docs/whatsnew/android-16-release)

[![](https://developer.android.com/images/picto-icons/flag.svg)](https://source.android.com/docs/setup/build/feature-flagging)

Featured

### [Feature launch flags](https://source.android.com/docs/setup/build/feature-flagging)

Android feature launch flags ensure that the AOSP development branch is stable for everyone. Contributors to AOSP can use feature launch flags to make sure only tested code is executed.



[Read more](https://source.android.com/docs/setup/build/feature-flagging)

Getting started

### [Setup](https://source.android.com/docs/setup)

Learn how to set up your environment, download the AOSP source, build Android, and make contributions.



Security

### [Security](https://source.android.com/docs/security)

Find out how Android incorporates industry-leading security features to keep the Android platform and ecosystem safe.



Core topics

### [Android fundamentals](https://source.android.com/docs/core)

From the basic building blocks of an Android device to the more intricate advanced features, learn about all the ways you can customize the Android OS.



Compatibility

### [Compatibility](https://source.android.com/docs/compatibility)

Ensure your users have a coherent experience as they use your device alongside other Android devices in the ecosystem.



Automotive

### [Automotive](https://source.android.com/docs/automotive)

Learn how to develop and customize the Android Automotive platform, which runs directly on in-vehicle hardware.



Devices

### [Android devices](https://source.android.com/docs/devices)

Read about creating configurable virtual Android devices, managing devices on a corporate network, and delivering live content to devices through Android TV.



[![](https://developer.android.com/images/picto-icons/security-2.svg)](https://source.android.com/docs/security/bulletin)

### [Security bulletins](https://source.android.com/docs/security/bulletin)

Help protect your device by checking the latest Android Security Bulletins. Also see the Android Automotive, Chromecast, Wear OS, Pixel, and Pixel Watch bulletins.



[![](https://developer.android.com/images/picto-icons/learn.svg)](https://source.android.com/docs/setup/start)

### [Android development](https://source.android.com/docs/setup/start)

Learn how to set up your environment, download the source, and start contributing to AOSP through this tutorial.



[![](https://developer.android.com/images/picto-icons/platform.svg)](https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture)

### [Architecture](https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture)

Learn how the pieces fit together, from the kernel to the HALs to updatable system components.



[![](https://developer.android.com/images/picto-icons/sync.svg)](https://source.android.com/docs/core/connect)

### [Connectivity](https://source.android.com/docs/core/connect)

Make sure your devices work together and are connected through Bluetooth, NFC, Wi-Fi, and telephony.
